LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JULY-02-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND STATES THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY SERVED. WELLS FARGO'S PROOF OF SERVICE STATES THAT ON APRIL 11, 2025, IT SERVED THE MOTION ON PLAINTIFF VIA ELECTRONIC SERVICE. EIGHTY-ONE DAYS FROM APRIL 11 IS JULY 1, 2025. THE MOTION WAS SERVED ELECTRONICALLY, WHICH EXTENDS THE TIME FOR HEARING BY TWO COURT DAYS TO JULY 3, 2025. (SEE CCP 1010.6(A)(3)(B).) JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-15-27 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2025 Continued To Feb-17-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Jul-02-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Jonathan Vaknin, Esq. of Vaknin Law Firm (tel: 510-480-1430) specially appearing for the Plaintiff.
Atty. Jenna Moreno-Cortez, Esq. of Law Offices of Robert S. Gitmeid, P.C. (tel: 909-443-7174) for the Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter discussed. Plaintiff's counsel requests a continuance. The Court denies the request, as the trial date is approaching. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling and states the motion is denied without prejudice.

The motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely served. Wells Fargo's proof of service states that on April 11, 2025, it served the motion on plaintiff via electronic service. Eighty-one days from April 11 is July 1, 2025. The motion was served electronically, which extends the time for hearing by two court days to July 3, 2025. (See CCP 1010.6(a)(3)(B).)

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
